summary: Replaced the directory-only `[ -d "$GIT_ROOT/.git" ]` test in Tools/generate-build-info.sh with `git -C "$GIT_ROOT" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1`, so the build-info stamp script recognizes git worktrees (where .git is a file pointer) as real repositories and stamps the actual commit, branch, and tag instead of falling through to the no-git fallback. Verified directly that the worktree case, the normal-checkout case, and the truly-non-git fallback case all behave correctly.
validations_passed: Direct stamp verification (worktree): SRCROOT=<worktree>/owlory_xcode bash Tools/generate-build-info.sh produced GitCommit=54df0d5cd2dd-dirty, GitBranch=claude/frosty-greider-e0a33c, GitTag=54df0d5-dirty (was no-git/no-git/no-git before the fix).
Direct stamp verification (normal checkout): SRCROOT=<main>/owlory_xcode bash Tools/generate-build-info.sh produced GitCommit=ba44c58834e5-dirty, GitBranch=main, GitStatus=dirty (still works).

Direct stamp verification (non-git fallback): SRCROOT=/tmp/not-a-repo bash Tools/generate-build-info.sh produced GitCommit=no-git, GitBranch=no-git, GitStatus=unknown (fallback preserved for truly non-git contexts).
recommended_next_reason: With the worktree stamping fix landed, a fresh device build from the post-fix commit can carry traceable provenance and the previously blocked write-promotion-device-verification slice can be retried. The old device install with no-git provenance must be overwritten by a fresh post-fix build before any on-device flow is exercised for proof.

contract_status_changes.before: Tools/generate-build-info.sh:36 used `[ -d "$GIT_ROOT/.git" ]`, which fails in any git worktree because .git is a file pointer (`gitdir: /path/to/main/.git/worktrees/<name>`) rather than a directory. The script then fell through to the no-git fallback (lines 47-54) and stamped GitCommit/GitCommitFull/GitBranch/GitTag as no-git, breaking BuildInfo.isReleaseable and source traceability for any worktree build.
contract_status_changes.after: The git-detection now uses `git -C "$GIT_ROOT" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1`, which succeeds for normal checkouts, worktrees, and submodules alike. A normal-checkout build still stamps the real commit/branch; a worktree build now stamps the real commit/branch instead of no-git; and a truly non-git SRCROOT still hits the documented no-git fallback. The comment block at lines 31-35 was updated to explain why the directory-only test was insufficient.
proof: Direct stamp script invocation against this worktree, the main checkout, and a non-git /tmp directory; output captured in validations_passed.

residual_risks: The fix only changes git detection. It does not add a regression test for the stamping behavior across repo shapes, so a future refactor of generate-build-info.sh could reintroduce a worktree-incompatible check without CI catching it. Adding such a test would be a separate slice in the repo-tooling domain.
BuildInfo.isReleaseable still requires a clean working tree in addition to a known commit; this fix does not change release gating, only the stamping inputs that release gating reads.
The previously installed Owlory build on the connected iPhone still carries no-git provenance and must be overwritten by a fresh post-fix install before any device-verified flow is exercised.
